manifest.price_change: Percentage price change in the period. Positive = price up, negative = price down.
manifest.price: Last traded price for the symbol.
manifest.fr: Funding rate of perpetual futures as a percentage value. Positive = longs pay shorts, negative = shorts pay longs.
manifest.oi: Open Interest in USD. Total size of open positions in the futures market. Increase = new positions entering, decrease = positions being closed.
manifest.oi_trend: Normalized angle of the exponential trend curve over Open Interest. Positive = OI growing, negative = OI declining.
manifest.lsr: Long/Short Ratio of top traders. Below 1 = short dominance, above 1 = long dominance.
manifest.lsr_trend: Normalized angle of the exponential trend curve over Long/Short Ratio. Positive = more longs opening, negative = more shorts opening.
manifest.trades: Number of trades executed in the period. Higher values indicate high activity/interest.
manifest.rsi: Relative Strength Index. 0-30 = oversold, 70-100 = overbought, 30-70 = neutral.
manifest.exp_btc: Normalized angle of the exponential price trend curve paired in BTC. Positive = gaining strength against BTC, negative = losing strength against BTC.
manifest.range_level: Accumulation strength level. Higher values indicate longer accumulation and stronger potential breakout. 0 = no accumulation detected.
manifest.trades_minute: Trade frequency per minute, normalized by timeframe duration. Raw count — useful to compare burst speed vs steady flow pressure on the same scale.
manifest.trades_second: Trade frequency per second, normalized by timeframe duration. Raw count — ideal for comparing high activity bursts against steady flow pressure.
manifest.exp: Normalized angle of the exponential price trend curve in USD. Positive = uptrend, negative = downtrend. Higher absolute value = stronger trend.
manifest.trades_level: Trade activity spike level compared to recent baseline. 0 = no spike. Higher values = larger deviation from normal activity. Color reflects whether the candle closed up (bullish) or down (bearish).
